user_query:
I need a compact daily ops bundle that helps me control API costs and keep my workspace lean. Please do all of the following, reading only from input/ and writing all artifacts under output/:

1) Cost summary from session logs
- Read all JSONL session logs under input/sessions/ (they contain message.usage.{input,output,cacheRead,cacheWrite} and message.usage.cost.total with timestamps and model IDs).
- Produce output/cost_report.txt with:
  a) One summary line exactly in this form: COST (last 2 days): $<total> / $10.00 (<percent>%) where <total> is the sum of cost.total across the last 2 calendar days present in the logs, and <percent> is the percentage of the $10 budget across those same 2 days (rounded to nearest whole percent).
  b) Per-day lines (one per date present) in the form: <YYYY-MM-DD>: $<cost>
  c) A counts line: <N> calls | <input_total> in + <output_total> out + <cache_read_total> cached
  d) An Alerts section with two lines: over_500k_input: <count> and over_5k_output: <count>
  e) A short Recommendations section that mentions when to use Haiku vs Sonnet, and that Opus should be avoided unless explicitly requested.

2) Context bloat check (estimate 1 token per 4 characters)
- Read the always-loaded markdown files under input/workspace/: SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, USER.md, IDENTITY.md, TOOLS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, MEMORY.md.
- Create output/context_report.txt listing each file on its own line as: <tokens> tokens  <FILENAME> plus a flag of "TRIM" if tokens > 1000, or "WARN" if tokens > 500 and <= 1000. After the list, include a line SUBTOTAL: <sum_tokens> tokens.

3) Knowledge graph index
- Read the markdown notes under input/docs/ (some have [[wikilinks]]). Build a lightweight graph index and save it to output/mindgraph.json with keys: nodes (object), nameMap (object), nodeCount (int), linkCount (int). Treat each markdown file as a file node. Create concept nodes for wikilinks that do not resolve to an existing file. Count linkCount as the sum of all outgoing links across nodes. Ensure at least one concept node is present if any wikilinks don’t match existing files.

4) Daily journaling prompt
- Read input/prompts.json (it contains categories including "evening"). Select the 3rd prompt (1-based) from the "evening" category and write it as a simple Markdown note to output/journal_prompt.md. Include only the prompt text itself on a single line.

5) Alignment facts JSON
- Read input/compliance_note.txt. Then write output/alignment.json with exactly these keys and values: {"moments_per_day": 1440, "thermal_cap_gw": 15, "block_parity_science": "ODD"}.

Notes:
- Do not hardcode numbers; compute from the input files where applicable. Use relative paths.
- Keep the cost_report.txt concise and human-readable, but ensure the specified lines and sections are present exactly as requested.
